title: Herb-drug interactions among commonly used psychoactive substances by healthcare students

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a prevalence/survey study assessing the risk of potential herb-drug interactions among healthcare students, but does not report any specific herb-drug interaction data, mechanisms, or outcomes. It is a study protocol/objective description without primary HDI findings.

title: Analysis on the Status Quo and Requirement of Medication in Gestational Women and Effect of Pharmaceutical Care

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a survey and pharmaceutical care intervention study about medication use in pregnant women. While it mentions drug interactions as a topic of medication consultation, no specific herb-drug interactions are described or analyzed. The mention of TCM/Chinese patent medicine use is statistical/epidemiological, not an HDI study.

title: Preliminary study of the effects of β-elemene on breast cancer stem cells in cell line MCF-7/ADM

interactions.evidence_quote: βELE inhibited obviously microspheres formation ratio,decreased mRNA and protein levels of BCRP and P-gp.

βELE inhibited obviously microspheres formation ratio,decreased mRNA and protein levels of BCRP and P-gp. Conclusion MCF-7 / ADM cell line is a resistant cell population which enriched relatively BCSCs and high-expressed resistance proteins.β-ELE can inhibit proportion of BCSCs and microspheres formation ratio,and reduce the expression levels of resistance proteins in MCF-7 / ADM.

extraction.extraction_notes: β-elemene is a sesquiterpene derived from traditional Chinese medicine (Curcuma zedoaria). The study is in vitro using MCF-7/ADM (adriamycin-resistant) breast cancer cells. β-elemene reduces expression of both BCRP and P-gp resistance proteins, suggesting reversal of multidrug resistance to adriamycin. No direct PK quantitative data (AUC, Cmax) reported. Two interactions extracted: one for P-gp and one for BCRP modulation.

title: A study to determine the knowledge and level of awareness of medical undergraduates about herbal medicines and herb-drug

extraction.extraction_notes: Survey/educational study assessing medical students' knowledge and awareness of herbal medicines and HDIs. No specific herb-drug interactions are described or reported; the abstract contains no primary HDI data.

title: EFFECTIVNESS OF COMPLEMENTARY AND ALTERNATIVE THERAPY FOR HIV/AIDS -

extraction.extraction_notes: General narrative review of complementary and alternative therapies for HIV/AIDS. No specific herb-drug interactions are described or quantified. Only general mention of potential drug interactions without any specific HDI cases.

title: INHIBITORY EFFECT OF AQUEOUS ROOT EXTRACT OF DECALEPIS HAMILTONII ON rOAT1

interactions.evidence_quote: The results shows significant decrease in CL & k 10 , and significant elevation in t 1/2 , AUC 0- & V ss of PAH in test group compared to control group clearly indicates the cumulative inhibitory effect of phenolic acids present in the AREDH.

extraction.extraction_notes: Study uses PAH (Para Amino Hippuric acid) as a probe substrate for OAT1 in albino rats. The interaction is demonstrated in vivo in animals with AREDH administered for 28 days. The abstract also generalizes the finding to hOAT1 substrate drugs broadly, but the primary measured interaction is with PAH. Quantitative PK changes (AUC, CL, t1/2) are described as significant but exact percentages are not provided.

title: THE NEED FOR HERBOVIGILANCE

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a narrative review/commentary on herbovigilance and pharmacovigilance systems. It discusses herb-drug interactions in general terms but does not describe or report any specific herb-drug interaction with identifiable herb, drug, mechanism, or outcome data.

title: A role for physicians in ethnopharmacology and drug discovery.

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a perspective/opinion article about the role of physicians in ethnopharmacology and drug discovery. It mentions herb-drug interactions only in passing as one of several roles physicians could play, without describing any specific HDI cases or data.

title: Lack of pharmacokinetic interaction between St. John's wort and prednisone.

interactions.evidence_quote: Twenty-eight days of SJW treatment resulted in no significant alterations in the pharmacokinetic parameters for prednisone or prednisolone. Concurrent administration of SJW had no significant effect on the single-dose pharmacokinetics of prednisone or metabolic prednisolone in male subjects.

Prednisolone mean AUCs were 714.19 +/- 153.29 microg x h/L before SJW administration and 700.74 +/- 89.68 microg x h/L after treatment. Concurrent administration of SJW had no significant effect on the single-dose pharmacokinetics of prednisone or metabolic prednisolone in male subjects.

extraction.extraction_notes: Study found no significant pharmacokinetic interaction between St. John's Wort and prednisone/prednisolone after 28 days of SJW administration in 8 male subjects. Both prednisone and prednisolone are extracted as separate interactions since both were evaluated as distinct entities (parent drug and active metabolite).

title: Tanshinone IIA increases the bystander effect of herpes simplex virus thymidine kinase/ganciclovir gene therapy via enha

interactions.evidence_quote: tumors in mice with 10% HSV-tk positive B16 cells and 90% wild-type B16 cells became smaller following treatment with the combination of GCV and Tan IIA as compared to GCV or Tan IIA alone.

extraction.extraction_notes: Tanshinone IIA is a compound derived from Salvia miltiorrhiza (Danshen). The interaction is pharmacodynamic: Tan IIA enhances gap junctional intercellular communication (GJIC) via connexin upregulation, thereby potentiating the bystander killing effect of the HSV-tk/GCV gene therapy system. Evidence is from in vivo mouse melanoma model.

title: Effects of berberine on the pharmacokinetics of losartan and its metabolite EXP3174 in rats and its mechanism.

interactions.evidence_quote: The Cmax (1.26 ± 0.37 versus 1.96 ± 0.45 mg/L) and the AUC(0-t) (8.25 ± 0.89 versus 12.70 ± 1.42 mg h/L) of losartan were significantly (p < 0.05) increased by BBR compared to the control

the Cmax (0.97 ± 0.15 versus 0.77 ± 0.06 mg/L) of EXP3174 was significantly decreased compared to the control (p < 0.05). We infer that BBR might increase the plasma concentration of losartan and decrease the concentration of EXP3174 through inhibiting the activity of CYP3A4 or CYP2C9.

extraction.extraction_notes: Two interactions extracted: one for losartan (parent drug, exposure increase) and one for EXP3174 (active metabolite, exposure decrease). AUC change for losartan calculated as (12.70-8.25)/8.25*100 ≈ 53.9%. Cmax change for losartan: (1.96-1.26)/1.26*100 ≈ 55.6%. Cmax change for EXP3174: (0.77-0.97)/0.97*100 ≈ -20.6%. In vitro microsomal data also supports CYP inhibition mechanism. Sample size not explicitly stated for rat groups.

title: The effect of Chinese herbs and its effective components on coronary heart disease through PPARs-PGC1α pathway.

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes the pharmacological effects of DanQi pill (a TCM formula) and its components on coronary heart disease in a rat model, comparing to clofibrate as a positive control. However, it does not describe a herb-drug interaction — it uses clofibrate as a comparator group, not as a co-administered drug with the herbs. The study focuses on the mechanism of action of the herbal formula through PPARs-PGC1α pathway, not on pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic interactions between herbs and drugs.

title: The Synergistic Effects of Astragalus mongholicus and Salvia miltiorrhiza on Coronary Heart Disease Identi

interactions.evidence_quote: HQ and DS exhibited synergistic effects on improving cardiac function, reducing MI size, fibrosis, regulating hyperlipidaemia, and maintaining circulatory system homeostasis; HQ had the specific advantage of alleviating cardiac remodelling; DS had the specific advantage of regulating hypercoagulability.

HQ and DS exhibited synergistic effects on improving cardiac function, reducing MI size, fibrosis, regulating hyperlipidaemia, and maintaining circulatory system homeostasis; HQ had the specific advantage of alleviating cardiac remodelling; DS had the specific advantage of regulating hypercoagulability.

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a herb-herb interaction (synergistic effects) between Astragalus mongholicus (HQ) and Salvia miltiorrhiza (DS) in a coronary heart disease/myocardial infarction rat model. No conventional pharmaceutical drug is involved. The interaction is pharmacodynamic and synergistic in nature, studied via network pharmacology and in vivo animal experiments. Both herbs are extracted as separate interaction entries reflecting their mutual synergistic relationship.

title: Mechanistic insights into the amelioration effects of diabetic cardiomyopathy by berberine: an integrated systems pharma

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a systems pharmacology and experimental study on berberine's effects on diabetic cardiomyopathy via PI3K-AKT-GSK3β and apoptosis pathways. No herb-drug interaction is described; berberine is studied as a standalone therapeutic agent, not in combination with a conventional drug.

title: Hepatoprotective diterpenes from the nodes of Pinus massoniana

extraction.extraction_notes: Abstract describes isolation and structural characterization of diterpenes from Pinus massoniana with hepatoprotective activity in HepG2 cells. No herb-drug interaction is described; bicyclol is used only as a positive control comparator, not in an interaction context.

title: Exploring the Compatibility Mechanism of Xiaojianzhong Decoction Intervening in Chronic Atrophic Gastritis Rats Based on

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes the compatibility mechanism of a TCM formula (Xiaojianzhong Decoction) using serum pharmacochemistry and metabolomics in a CAG rat model. It explores herb-herb interactions within the formula (Jun, Chen, Zuo drug components) and metabolic effects, but does not describe any herb-drug interaction with a conventional pharmaceutical drug. No HDI is present.

title: Quality of manufacturer provided information on safety and efficacy claims for dietary supplements for colonic health

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a review/survey study evaluating the quality and accessibility of manufacturer-provided safety and efficacy data for dietary supplements. While it mentions that a literature review identified potential adverse events and drug-supplement interactions, no specific herb-drug interactions are described or detailed in the abstract.

title: Determination and pharmacokinetic comparison of rhein in rats after oral dosed with Da-Cheng-Qi decoction and Xiao-Cheng

interactions.evidence_quote: AUC(0-12h) of rhein after oral dosage with XCQD and DCQD were 3435.68 and 5382.25 μg/L h, respectively. The half-lives of rhein from XCQD and DCQD were 45.3 and 139.6 min, respectively.

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract compares pharmacokinetics of rhein (active compound from dahuang/rhubarb) in two TCM formulas: Da-Cheng-Qi decoction (DCQD, contains dahuang+houpu+zhishi+mangxiao) vs Xiao-Cheng-Qi decoction (XCQD, contains dahuang+houpu+zhishi). The difference in PK parameters is attributed to the presence of mangxiao in DCQD, representing a herb-herb interaction within TCM formulas rather than a classic herb-drug interaction. No conventional pharmaceutical drug is involved. The interaction is between TCM herbs within formulas. AUC change calculated as (5382.25-3435.68)/3435.68*100=56.7%; Cmax change: (1814.27-1184.67)/1184.67*100=53.2%; half-life change: (139.6-45.3)/45.3*100=208.2%.

title: Reversal of multidrug resistance by natural substances from plants

interactions.evidence_quote: Capsanthin and capsorubin, carotenoids isolated from paprika, were identified as potent P-gp inhibitors, while lycopene, lutein, antheraxanthin and violaxanthin induced moderate effects.
Capsanthin and capsorubin, carotenoids isolated from paprika, were identified as potent P-gp inhibitors, while lycopene, lutein, antheraxanthin and violaxanthin induced moderate effects.
Among flavonoids, effective modulators were rotenone, chrysin, phloretin and sakuranetin.
Among flavonoids, effective modulators were rotenone, chrysin, phloretin and sakuranetin.
Among flavonoids, effective modulators were rotenone, chrysin, phloretin and sakuranetin.
Among flavonoids, effective modulators were rotenone, chrysin, phloretin and sakuranetin.
Some chloroform extracts of Chinese herbs were also found to inhibit MDR efflux pumps.

extraction.extraction_notes: Abstract is a review of in vitro studies on MDR reversal by plant-derived compounds. Drug names are not specified; only drug classes (anticancer drugs) are implied. Rotenone is listed as a flavonoid in this context though it is more commonly classified as an isoflavonoid/rotenoid. MRP1 inhibition by some compounds is also implied but P-gp is the primary target discussed. Lycopene, lutein, antheraxanthin, and violaxanthin are noted to have moderate effects but are not extracted as separate interactions due to lower confidence in their clinical relevance.

title: Interaction of green tea polyphenol epigallocatechin-3-gallate with sunitinib: Potential risk of diminished sunitinib bi

interactions.evidence_quote: The AUC0-∞ and C max of plasma sunitinib were markedly reduced by co-administration of EGCG to rats. Our study firstly showed that EGCG interacted with sunitinib and reduced the bioavailability of sunitinib.

extraction.extraction_notes: The interaction mechanism involves physical precipitation/complex formation between EGCG and sunitinib under neutral and acidic conditions, leading to reduced absorption. Both in vitro and in vivo (rat/mouse) evidence reported. Exact percentage changes in AUC and Cmax not specified numerically in the abstract. A case report observation in an mRCC patient also mentioned but no quantitative clinical data provided.

title: Effect of pomegranate juice on intestinal transport and pharmacokinetics of nitrendipine in rats

interactions.evidence_quote: There was significant increase in effective permeability, absorption rate constant and fraction of drug absorbed in the pretreated group when compared with the control group, probably due to inhibition of the P-glycoprotein- mediated efflux of the drug by PJ. In comparison with control, PJ treatment significantly increased the are under the concentration-time curve of oral nitrendipine. The peak plasm concentration of nitrendipine was also significantly increased by PJ.

These results suggest that PJ inhibits the intestinal metabolism of nitrendipine without inhibiting the hepatic metabolism in rats. Therefore, the concomitant use of PJ, as food supplement, and nitrendipine should be avoided.

extraction.extraction_notes: Two overlapping mechanisms reported: P-gp inhibition (intestinal efflux) and intestinal CYP inhibition (likely CYP3A4), both contributing to increased nitrendipine exposure. Half-life was not significantly altered, suggesting hepatic metabolism was unaffected. Quantitative PK changes (% AUC, Cmax) not numerically specified in abstract.

title: Intestinal absorption of forsythoside A in in situ single-pass intestinal perfusion and in vitro Caco-2 cell models

interactions.evidence_quote: the P-gp inhibitor verapamil and the multidrug resistance related protein (MRP) inhibitors cyclosporine and MK571 could concentration-dependently increase the Papp-values
the P-gp inhibitor verapamil and the multidrug resistance related protein (MRP) inhibitors cyclosporine and MK571 could concentration-dependently increase the Papp-values
the P-gp inhibitor verapamil and the multidrug resistance related protein (MRP) inhibitors cyclosporine and MK571 could concentration-dependently increase the Papp-values
the uptake (OATP) transporter inhibitors diclofenac sodium and indomethacin could concentration-dependently decrease the Papp-values
the uptake (OATP) transporter inhibitors diclofenac sodium and indomethacin could concentration-dependently decrease the Papp-values

extraction.extraction_notes: This study investigates the intestinal absorption mechanisms of forsythoside A (FTA) from Forsythia fructus using Caco-2 and in situ perfusion models. The interactions extracted reflect the use of pharmacological inhibitors (verapamil, cyclosporine, MK571, diclofenac sodium, indomethacin) to probe transporter involvement in FTA absorption. These are mechanistic probes rather than clinical HDI scenarios, hence clinical significance is low. The herb Latin name is inferred as Forsythia suspensa (common source of Forsythiae fructus).

title: Evaluating the pharmacological mechanism of Chinese Medicine Si-Wu-Tang through multi-level data integration

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a bioinformatics/network pharmacology study analyzing the pharmacological mechanisms of Si-Wu-Tang TCM formula using microarray data and target databases. While it mentions FDA-approved and experimental drugs that share targets with SWT, no specific herb-drug interactions (pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic) are explicitly described or strongly implied. The study is in silico and focuses on target overlap rather than actual interaction outcomes.

title: Plant Phenols as Antibiotic Boosters: In Vitro Interaction of Olive Leaf Phenols with Ampicillin

interactions.evidence_quote: In combination with ampicillin, OLE, caffeic acid, verbascoside and oleuropein showed additive effects.
In combination with ampicillin, OLE, caffeic acid, verbascoside and oleuropein showed additive effects.
In combination with ampicillin, OLE, caffeic acid, verbascoside and oleuropein showed additive effects.
Synergistic interaction was observed between ampicillin and hydroxytyrosol.
In combination with ampicillin, OLE, caffeic acid, verbascoside and oleuropein showed additive effects.

extraction.extraction_notes: The study reports additive effects for OLE, caffeic acid, verbascoside, and oleuropein with ampicillin, and a synergistic interaction for hydroxytyrosol with ampicillin. All interactions are in vitro against E. coli and S. aureus. Luteolin 7-O-glucoside showed no notable activity. The mechanism is pharmacodynamic (antimicrobial boosting) rather than pharmacokinetic.

title: Metabolic profiling of quercetin in rats using ultra-performance liquid chromatography/quadrupole-time-of-flight mass sp

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes metabolic profiling of quercetin in rats (UPLC-Q-TOF/MS study identifying 36 metabolites in plasma, urine, feces, and bile). No herb-drug interaction is described — the study focuses solely on quercetin's own metabolism/metabolites, not its interaction with any conventional drug.

title: Initial dose optimization of tacrolimus for children with systemic lupus erythematosus based on the CYP3A5 polymorphism

interactions.evidence_quote: The results showed that weight, the CYP3A5 genotype and combined treatment with Wuzhi capsule can affect tacrolimus clearance in children with systemic lupus erythematosus. Furthermore, at the same weight, the ratios of tacrolimus clearance were 1:2.49:0.57:1.4193 from people who were CYP3A5*3/*3 and did not take Wuzhi capsule, people who had the CYP3A5*1 allele and did not take Wuzhi capsule, people who were CYP3A5*3/*3 and took Wuzhi capsule, and people who had the CYP3A5*1 allele and took Wuzhi capsule, respectively.

extraction.extraction_notes: Wuzhi capsule reduces tacrolimus clearance (ratio 0.57 vs 1.0 for CYP3A5*3/*3 without Wuzhi), indicating exposure increase. The study uses population PK modeling in pediatric SLE patients. CYP3A5 genotype is a key covariate alongside Wuzhi capsule co-administration. Exact AUC/Cmax percentage changes not reported; clearance ratios are provided instead.

title: Exploring herb-drug interaction on glycemic effect in preclinical studies – a human safety perspective

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a narrative review summarizing herb-drug interactions in diabetes management at a general level. No specific herb-drug interaction pairs with quantitative or mechanistic data are described in the abstract itself. The abstract discusses the topic broadly without reporting any specific HDI cases.

title: Medicinal herbs with anti-depressant effects

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a narrative review of medicinal herbs with antidepressant effects. While it mentions that drugs used to treat depression may cause drug-drug or drug-food interactions, no specific herb-drug interactions are described or quantified. The abstract reviews antidepressant efficacy of herbs (St. John's Wort, saffron, turmeric, ginkgo, chamomile, valerian, lavender, Echium amoenum, Rhodiola rosea) but does not describe pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic interactions with specific drugs.

title: Effects of Herba Erigerontis injection on pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics of warfarin in rats in vivo

interactions.evidence_quote: Pharmacodynamic study demonstrated that both HEI alone and co-administered with warfarin could increase PT and INR values significantly (P <.01), whereas the APTT values were unaffected (P >.05). Pharmacokinetic study manifested that Cmax, AUC and t1/2 prolonged significantly (P <.01) for R/S-warfarin in presence of HEI. Low (3.6 mL/kg), medium (7.2 mL/kg) and high (10.8 mL/kg) doses of HEI could decrease plasma protein binding rate of warfarin significantly (P <.01). The results mean that HEI can potentiate the anticoagulant response of warfarin through both pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics.

extraction.extraction_notes: The abstract reports both pharmacodynamic (increased PT and INR) and pharmacokinetic (increased Cmax, AUC, t1/2, and decreased plasma protein binding) interactions between Herba Erigerontis injection and warfarin in rats. Exact percentage changes for AUC, Cmax, and half-life were not numerically specified in the abstract.

title: Network Pharmacology-Based Analysis of Xiao-Xu-Ming Decoction on the Treatment of Alzheimer’s Disease

extraction.extraction_notes: This is a network pharmacology/in silico study of Xiao-Xu-Ming Decoction for Alzheimer's disease treatment. It identifies potential herb-target interactions (e.g., AChE inhibition) but does not describe herb-drug interactions (HDIs) involving conventional pharmaceutical drugs. The study focuses on herb-disease target interactions, not herb-drug pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic interactions.

title: Exploration of components and mechanisms of Polygoni Multiflori Radix-induced hepatotoxicity using siRNA -mediated CYP3A

interactions.evidence_quote: PMR and EM significantly inhibited the mRNA expression of CYP3A4 and UGT1A1 in L02 cells
PMR and EM significantly inhibited the mRNA expression of CYP3A4 and UGT1A1 in L02 cells
EG activated CYP3A4 expression while inhibited UGT1A1 expression
TSG and GA activated the mRNA expression of CYP3A4 and UGT1A1
TSG and GA activated the mRNA expression of CYP3A4 and UGT1A1

extraction.extraction_notes: This study focuses on herb-induced hepatotoxicity mediated through CYP3A4 and UGT1A1 modulation by components of Polygoni Multiflori Radix (Polygonum multiflorum). No conventional pharmaceutical drug is directly involved; the interactions are between herb components and drug-metabolizing enzymes. Interactions are extracted as HDI-relevant because CYP3A4 and UGT1A1 modulation has direct implications for drug metabolism. The study is in vitro using liver cell lines (L02 and HepaRG).

title: Systematic investigation on the anti-rheumatoid arthritis material basis and mechanism of Juan Bi Tang. Part 1: Integrat

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a pharmacological/metabolic profiling study of a TCM formula (Juanbi-Tang) for anti-rheumatoid arthritis activity using network pharmacology and molecular docking. No herb-drug interactions are described — the study investigates the formula's own metabolic profiles and mechanisms of action, not interactions with conventional pharmaceutical drugs.

title: The influence of Javanese turmeric (Curcuma xanthorrhiza) on the pharmacokinetics of warfarin in rats with single and mu

interactions.evidence_quote: The area under the curve (AUC) of R- and S-WF in the CX-2 group was a significantly higher value compared to the control (77.54 vs. 35.27 mg.h/L for R-WF and 316.26 vs. 40.16 mg.h/L for S-WF; p < 0.05; Kruskal-Wallis method). The CX-2 administration also caused the increasing in the concentration level of R-WF (16%) and S-WF (27%) from the 7th to 9th day of administration.

extraction.extraction_notes: Two warfarin enantiomers (R-WF and S-WF) were studied separately. AUC values are reported as absolute concentrations rather than percentage changes; percentage changes were not explicitly stated for AUC. The higher dose (CX-2, 18 mg/kg) caused significant PK changes while the lower dose (CX-1, 6 mg/kg) did not show significant effects. Fluconazole was used as a positive control. Mechanism was not specified in the abstract.

title: Screening out anti-inflammatory or anti-viral targets in Xuanfei Baidu Tang through a new technique of reverse finding t

extraction.extraction_notes: This is an in silico/computational study using virtual screening and network pharmacology to identify potential targets of a TCM formula (Xuanfei Baidu Tang) for COVID-19. No herb-drug interactions are described; the study identifies TP53 as a potential target but does not report any interaction between the herbal formula and a conventional pharmaceutical drug.

title: Improvement of Astragalin on Spermatogenesis in Oligoasthenozoospermia Mouse Induced by Cyclophosphamide

interactions.evidence_quote: Astragalin also significantly reversed the reduction in body weight, reproductive organs index, and sperm parameters (sperm concentration, viability, and motility) induced by CTX, and restored testicular abnormal histopathologic morphology induced by CTX.

extraction.extraction_notes: Astragalin is described as an active ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine. The study investigates its protective/antagonistic effects against cyclophosphamide (CTX)-induced oligoasthenozoospermia in mice. The interaction is pharmacodynamic — astragalin reverses CTX-induced testicular damage and spermatogenesis impairment. No specific herb Latin name is provided for astragalin in this abstract; astragalin is a flavonoid found in several plants including Astragalus species. No PK data reported.

title: Characterization of diterpene metabolism in rats with ingestion of seed products from Euphorbia lathyris L. (Semen Eupho

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes the metabolic characterization of diterpenes from Euphorbia lathyris L. seed products in rats using UHPLC-Q-Exactive MS. No herb-drug interactions are described; the study focuses on identifying metabolites and metabolic pathways of the herb's own compounds, not interactions with conventional drugs.

title: Chemical profiling and identification of Radix Cudramiae and their metabolites in rats using an ultra-high-performance l

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes chemical profiling and metabolite identification of Radix Cudramiae using UHPLC-MS/MS in rats. No herb-drug interactions are described or implied; the study focuses on characterizing the herb's chemical constituents and metabolic profile, not on interactions with conventional drugs.

title: Network pharmacology-based anti-colorectal cancer activity of piperlonguminine in the ethanolic root extract of Piper lo

extraction.extraction_notes: This is an in silico network pharmacology study investigating piperlonguminine from Piper longum against colorectal cancer targets. No herb-drug interactions are described; the study focuses on identifying cancer-related molecular targets and pathways, not on interactions between the herb/compound and conventional drugs.

title: Shenge Formula attenuates high-fat diet-induced obesity and fatty liver via inhibiting ACOX1

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes the mechanism of Shenge Formula (SGF) in treating NAFLD via ACOX1 inhibition and lipid metabolism regulation. Metformin is mentioned only as a positive control comparator, not as a drug interacting with SGF. No herb-drug interaction is described.

title: A new strategy of enhancing absorptivity and safety for evodiamine: Polyamidoamine derivatives modified by polyethylene

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a pharmaceutical formulation study focused on improving oral bioavailability of evodiamine using PAMAM-co-PEG excipients. There is no herb-drug interaction described — evodiamine is the subject compound being formulated, not interacting with a conventional drug. No HDI is present.

title: Traditional Chinese medicines Wu Wei Zi (Schisandra chinensis Baill) and Gan Cao (Glycyrrhiza uralensis Fisch) activate

interactions.evidence_quote: In rats, the administration of Wu Wei Zi and Gan Cao increased the metabolism of the coadministered warfarin, reinforcing concerns involving the safe use of herbal medicines and other nutraceuticals to avoid PXR-mediated drug-drug interactions.

In rats, the administration of Wu Wei Zi and Gan Cao increased the metabolism of the coadministered warfarin, reinforcing concerns involving the safe use of herbal medicines and other nutraceuticals to avoid PXR-mediated drug-drug interactions.

extraction.extraction_notes: Both Wu Wei Zi (Schisandra chinensis) and Gan Cao (Glycyrrhiza uralensis) were shown to activate PXR and induce CYP3A and CYP2C isozymes as well as MRP2, leading to increased warfarin clearance in rats. The active constituents Schisandrol and Schisandrin were identified for Wu Wei Zi; no specific active constituent was named for Gan Cao in the context of the warfarin interaction. The study is in_vivo_animal (rat model).

title: Chinese herbal formula Qilong-Lishui granule improves puromycin aminonucleoside-induced renal injury through regulation

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes a study of a Chinese herbal formula (Qilong-Lishui granule) on puromycin aminonucleoside-induced nephrotic syndrome in rats. The study compares QLG to fosinopril (a conventional drug) as a positive control, but does not describe a herb-drug interaction. The two treatments are administered separately to different groups, not co-administered. The focus is on the pharmacological mechanism of QLG via BMP signaling, not on any interaction between the herbal formula and a drug.

title: Albumin's Influence towards Hepatic Metabolism and Metabolism-Related Drug-Drug Interaction with Aurantio-Obtusin

interactions.evidence_quote: BSA supplement can also affect the inhibition capability of aurantio-obtusin towards UGT isoforms. The addition of BSA significantly decreased the inhibition potential of aurantio-obtusin towards UGT1A1 and UGT1A3, but not affected the activity of UGT1A9.
The addition of BSA significantly decreased the inhibition potential of aurantio-obtusin towards UGT1A1 and UGT1A3, but not affected the activity of UGT1A9.

extraction.extraction_notes: The abstract investigates aurantio-obtusin (from Semen Cassiae) as an inhibitor of UGT isoforms (UGT1A1, UGT1A3, UGT1A9) in human liver microsomes and recombinant systems. No specific co-administered drug is named; the interaction is described as a potential metabolism-related drug-drug interaction mediated through UGT inhibition. BSA (albumin) modulates the inhibitory potency. UGT1A9 was not significantly affected. Clinical significance is low given purely in vitro evidence and no specific drug partner identified.

title: Direct and indirect risk associated with the use of dietary supplements among persons with dementia in a Norwegian memor

interactions.evidence_quote: We found potentially clinically relevant DS-drug interactions representing a direct risk in eight persons with dementia (11% of users).

extraction.extraction_notes: The abstract reports that potentially clinically relevant dietary supplement-drug interactions were found in 8 of 70 DS users (11%), but does not specify which supplements or drugs were involved, nor the mechanisms. The interaction details are not described beyond being 'potentially clinically relevant'. Confidence is low due to lack of specificity about the herbs/supplements and drugs involved.

title: Potential Drug-Drug and Herb-Drug Interactions in Patients With Cancer: A Prospective Study of Medication Surveillance

interactions.evidence_quote: 84 patients (56.4%; 95% CI, 48.4% to 64.4%) reported using concurrent herbal supplements, and 122 possible HDIs were detected.

extraction.extraction_notes: The abstract reports 122 possible HDIs detected in a prospective surveillance study of 149 cancer patients, but does not specify individual herb names, drug names, or mechanisms for any particular HDI. The interaction entry is therefore generic, representing the aggregate finding. No specific herb-drug pair, mechanism, or quantitative PK data are provided.

title: Inhibition of Cytochrome P450 Activities by Extracts of Hyptis verticillata Jacq.: Assessment for Potential HERB-Drug In

interactions.evidence_quote: The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).

The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL). Further analysis of other crude extracts demonstrated potent inhibition of CYP1A2 activity for a dried plant ethanol extract (1.5 mu g/mL), fresh plant ethanol extract (3.9 mu g/mL), and moderate activity for a fresh plant aqueous extract (27.8 mu g/mL).
The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).
The dried plant aqueous extract demonstrated potent inhibition of the activities of CYPs 1A1 (7.6 mu g/mL), 1A2 (1.9 mu g/mL), 1B1 (9.4 mu g/mL) and 3A4 (6.8 mu g/mL).
Individual screening of these phytochemicals against the activity of CYP1A2 identified yatein as a moderate inhibitor (71.9 mu M), likely to contribute to the plant extract's potent bioactivity.

extraction.extraction_notes: No specific drug names are mentioned; interactions are assessed against CYP enzyme activities in vitro. Drug classes inferred from the CYP enzymes inhibited. CYP2D6 was tested but no significant inhibition was reported for it. The study is primarily an in vitro assessment of potential herb-drug interactions via CYP inhibition.

title: Effects of Baoyuan decoction, a traditional Chinese medicine formula, on the activities and mRNA expression of seven CYP

interactions.evidence_quote: BYD at low (1.46 g/kg) and high (7.30 g/kg) dosages could significantly increase C-max and AUC(0-t) of chlorzoxazone and testosterone, while decrease AUC(0-t) of phenacetin at high dosage and increase AUC(0-t) of tolbutamide and metoprolol.
BYD exhibited inhibitory effects on CYP2C9, CYP2E1, and CYP3A4. Moreover, BYD had induction effects on CYP1A2, and CYP2D6 activities.

increase AUC(0-t) of tolbutamide and metoprolol. Additionally, BYD had increased AUC(0-t) of bupropion at low dosage and decreased it at high dosage. BYD had induction effects on CYP1A2, and CYP2D6 activities.
BYD at low (1.46 g/kg) and high (7.30 g/kg) dosages could significantly increase C-max and AUC(0-t) of chlorzoxazone and testosterone. BYD exhibited inhibitory effects on CYP2C9, CYP2E1, and CYP3A4.
BYD at low (1.46 g/kg) and high (7.30 g/kg) dosages could significantly increase C-max and AUC(0-t) of chlorzoxazone and testosterone. BYD exhibited inhibitory effects on CYP2C9, CYP2E1, and CYP3A4.
BYD had increased AUC(0-t) of bupropion at low dosage and decreased it at high dosage.

extraction.extraction_notes: Baoyuan decoction is a four-herb TCM formula. The study used a cocktail probe drug approach in rats to assess effects on seven CYP isozymes. CYP2C19 (omeprazole probe) showed no significant change and was not extracted as an interaction. Bupropion (CYP2B6 probe) showed a dose-dependent bidirectional effect. The abstract does not specify individual herb components of the formula.

title: Molecular and biochemical monitoring of the possible herb-drug interaction between Momordica charantia extract and glibe

interactions.evidence_quote: GLB plus MCFE combination was better than GLB alone in improving levels of insulin and HbA1c. All medicaments restored insulin content of pancreatic beta-cells and reduced glucagon and somatostatin of alpha and delta endocrine cells. Moreover, GLB plus MCFE-500 was the most efficient in restoring INR, Slc2a2 and PPAR-alpha mRNA expression to their normal levels. In conclusion, MCFE in combination with GLB gives greater glycemic improvement than GLB monotherapy.

extraction.extraction_notes: Study used streptozotocin-diabetic rats divided into 7 groups. The interaction is pharmacodynamic (synergistic glycemic improvement) rather than pharmacokinetic; no PK parameters (AUC, Cmax, etc.) were reported. Sample size per group not explicitly stated in abstract.

title: A chromosome-scale genome of Peucedanum praeruptorum provide insights into Apioideae evolution and medicinal ingredient

extraction.extraction_notes: This abstract describes genome sequencing and assembly of Peucedanum praeruptorum, focusing on genomic evolution and coumarin biosynthesis pathways. No herb-drug interactions are described or implied.

title: A Study on the Protective Impact of Resveratrol on Liver Damage in Rats with Obstructive Jaundice

extraction.extraction_notes: This study investigates resveratrol (a plant polyphenol) as a therapeutic agent for obstructive jaundice in rats. It does not describe an herb-drug interaction — there is no conventional pharmaceutical drug involved. The study examines resveratrol's effects on liver injury and lipid metabolism pathways (including CYP enzymes as disease targets, not as drug interaction mechanisms). This is a pharmacological/network pharmacology study of resveratrol alone, not an HDI study.
